name: agent-karma
description: Local-first AI-coding validation & self-awareness coach. Did you verify what the AI produced before trusting it?

viewsWelcome.contents:
Track whether you validated what the AI produced — before you trusted it.

[▶ Start Session](command:agentKarma.startSession)
[■ End Session](command:agentKarma.endSession)
[✅ Log a Validation](command:agentKarma.addValidationCommand)
[📊 Open Dashboard](command:agentKarma.showDashboard)
[🏅 Generate Karma Card](command:agentKarma.generateKarmaCard)
[🩺 Check Validation Readiness](command:agentKarma.checkValidationReadiness)
[📤 Export as JSON](command:agentKarma.exportJson)
[📤 Export as Markdown](command:agentKarma.exportMarkdown)
[⚙ Configure Settings](command:agentKarma.openSettings)
[🗑 Reset Karma History](command:agentKarma.resetHistory)

Everything stays on your machine. [Learn more](https://github.com/AI-First-Community/Agent-Karma-Public#readme)

agentKarma.enabled.markdownDescription: Master switch. When off, Agent Karma stops **automatically** capturing anything — file saves and terminal validation commands are ignored, and ambient day-sessions won't start. You can still open the dashboard and your existing data. Turn off to pause all passive capture.

agentKarma.captureTerminalCommands.markdownDescription: Automatically detect validation commands (tests / build / lint / type-check) you run in the integrated terminal, via shell integration. Only the command **type** and result are kept — the raw command string is classified then discarded. When off, log validations manually with **Agent Karma: Add Validation Command** or `@agentkarma /verify`.

agentKarma.captureExternalFileChanges.markdownDescription:
Also capture file changes made **outside the editor** — by AI coding agents (e.g. Claude Code), CLI tools, scripts, or another editor — using a filesystem watcher, not just VS Code's save event. **Metadata only** (file name, never content), only while a session is active, and dependency/build/cache folders (`node_modules`, `dist`, `.git`, `coverage`, …) are ignored. Turn off to capture only edits you save in the editor.

Note: this records file **activity**. Your Karma **score** still reflects validation discipline — log tests/build/lint via **✅ Log a Validation** or `@agentkarma /verify` for work done outside the integrated terminal.

agentKarma.capturePromptText.markdownDescription: Store the **intent / prompt text** you enter when starting a session. The prompt-clarity score is always computed (it doesn't reveal the text); turning this off keeps the session but leaves the intent text blank. Local only — never sent anywhere.

agentKarma.enableGitDiffSummary.markdownDescription: At session end, compute a **git diff summary** — file/line **counts only**, never the diff content — to show how much changed. When off, sessions end without diff stats.

agentKarma.storeFullFilePath.markdownDescription: Store the **full workspace-relative path** of saved files in the session timeline. Off by default (only the file name is kept). Turn on if you want to see exactly which files changed.

agentKarma.idleEndMinutes.markdownDescription: If a recording session is recovered after a reload and has been idle longer than this many minutes, Agent Karma offers to end it (so a forgotten session doesn't run forever).

agentKarma.readClaudeUsage.markdownDescription: Read Claude Code's **local** session logs (`~/.claude/projects/…`) to show what your AI work cost — tokens, turns, model. Off by default. Fully local: no network, no API key, and only usage **metadata** is read (your prompts, the AI's replies, and your code are never read or stored).

agentKarma.cardName.markdownDescription: Name to print on your shareable **Karma Card**. Leave blank to use your local git `user.name` (or your OS username). Resolved locally — never sent anywhere.

chatParticipants.name: agentkarma
chatParticipants.description: Log that you validated the AI's work, or see your Karma summary.

commands.name: verify
commands.description: Log a validation you ran (tests/build/lint/types) — covers browser & copy-paste AI

commands.name: summary
commands.description: Your latest Karma, this week's nudge, and whether you can even validate

scripts.test:integration: npm run build && npm run compile-tests && node ./out/test/runTest.js
scripts.check:no-network: node -e "const fs=require('fs');const b=fs.readFileSync('dist/extension.js','utf8');const hits=[/\\bfetch\\s*\\(/, /require\\(['\"](https?|net|http2)['\"]\\)/, /from\\s+['\"](node:)?(https?|net|http2)['\"]/].filter(r=>r.test(b));if(hits.length){console.error('Network API found in bundle:',hits.map(String));process.exit(1)}console.log('OK: no network APIs in bundle')"
